mission.title: containerd: Set `[plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry].config_path` by default
mission.description: Set `[plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry].config_path` by default. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current containerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your containerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
helm list -n containerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working containerd installation.

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
### What is the problem you're trying to solve

I'm trying to add a new private registry (with a self-signed CA) to an existing Kubernetes cluster, where I may not have ssh access (but I do have `cluster-admin`).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Set `[plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry].config_p…
steps.description:
This is not defaulted because it is a new option which is replacing older options.
We could see if the older options are set and set a default if they are not, maybe?
Alternatively, depending on your situation, ask your k8s provider to set a default value for new nodes.
```yaml
echo "${PEM_ENCODED_CERT}" > /etc/docker/certs.d/${REGISTRY_NAME}/ca.crt
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Set `[plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry].config_p…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This is not defaulted because it is a new option which is replacing older options.
We could see if the older options are set and set a default if they are not, maybe?
Alternatively, depending on your situation, ask your k8s provider to set a default value for new nodes.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
